mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS sits near the zero lower bound, the discount rate applied to equity cash flows is structurally compressed, mechanically supporting valuations and producing a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 forward returns over the following month regardless of other conditions.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and 20-day forward SPX returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or returns are not significantly higher when FEDFUNDS < 1% versus historical baseline (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A cyclical peak in UNRATE followed by a decline of at least 0.2 percentage points relative to its trailing 6-month average signals the start of a labor market recovery, which precedes broad economic reacceleration and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls more than 0.2pp below its trailing 6-month moving average
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 9.5, elevated relative to historical norms, consistent with late-recession labor slack
failure_condition: In triggered instances, 60-day forward SPX returns are negative or statistically indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.10) across a 5-year out-of-sample walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in RSAFS (retail sales) and HOUST (housing starts) jointly signal broad-based consumer and investment demand recovery that is not captured by either series alone, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month; the effect requires both series to co-move, since either alone is noisy.
failure_condition: Joint RSAFS+HOUST positive momentum fails to outperform either single-series signal in out-of-sample Rank IC, or combined signal Rank IC < 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month growth in INDPRO reflects expanding industrial output and corporate earnings capacity, generating a short-horizon positive drift in S&P 500 returns over the following trading week as markets incorporate improving production data.
failure_condition: 5-day forward SPX returns following positive INDPRO growth prints are not significantly different from returns following negative prints (t-test p > 0.10) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a zero-lower-bound regime combined with subdued CPIAUCSL inflation, markets price in extended monetary accommodation, causing equities to rally over a quarterly horizon as investors reallocate from low-yield fixed income into risk assets; this mechanism is specific to ZLB conditions and does not generalize to normal-rate regimes.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.21 and CPIAUCSL at 214.459 consistent with a near-zero rate, low-inflation regime
regime_hint: zero lower bound / low inflation accommodation regime
failure_condition: During periods meeting the ZLB+low-inflation trigger, 60-day forward SPX returns are negative or show no significant positive skew relative to unconditional baseline (p > 0.10) across at least two distinct historical ZLB episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in PAYEMS exceeding 200,000 jobs signals a strengthening labor market that supports consumer spending and corporate revenue expectations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following month.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 131692 thousand persons, indicating a low post-recession employment base with substantial room for job gains
failure_condition: Triggered instances of PAYEMS gains above threshold produce 20-day forward returns not significantly greater than the unconditional mean (p > 0.10) over a 5-year walk-forward evaluation.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive quarter-over-quarter growth in GDPC1 reflects underlying real economic expansion that supports corporate earnings growth, producing a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon, independent of short-term market noise.
failure_condition: Rank IC between GDPC1 quarterly growth and 60-day forward SPX returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 3-year window, given GDPC1's high revision risk potentially distorting real-time signal quality.

mechanism_narrative: The joint condition of high UNRATE (labor slack) and near-zero FEDFUNDS indicates the central bank has substantial room and incentive to maintain accommodative policy, and their combination signals a policy-driven equity support mechanism stronger than either variable alone, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 9.5 and FEDFUNDS at 0.21 jointly indicate high slack with maximal policy accommodation
regime_hint: high unemployment paired with zero-rate policy regime
failure_condition: Joint UNRATE-FEDFUNDS trigger fails to outperform the single-series FEDFUNDS-only signal in out-of-sample Rank IC, or triggered-period 20-day returns show no significant positive bias (p > 0.10).
